model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: Michigan and Arizona are separated by less than one point in adjusted efficiency margin (KenPom #2 at +38.38 vs. #3 at +37.56, Torvik #2 vs. #3), with both teams posting identical 31-2 records and elite two-way profiles — Michigan's 2nd-ranked defense (AdjDE 88.4) marginally superior to Arizona's 3rd-ranked unit (AdjDE 89.7), and both offenses ranked 7th-8th nationally. The tiebreakers that tip this razor-thin pick to Michigan are the Wolverines' fractionally superior efficiency on both ends of the floor, the Big Ten's slight schedule-strength edge over the Big 12, and the pre-tournament betting market's consistent lean toward Michigan (-150 vs. Arizona -120 to reach the Final Four). Yaxel Lendeborg's ankle injury is a genuine concern but he has played through the tournament, while Koa Peat's return from his lower-body strain keeps Arizona fully dangerous — making this effectively a coin-flip game where Michigan's marginal efficiency edge and Dusty May's elite defensive system provide the narrowest of margins.

reasoningStep.title: Arizona vs. Michigan Final Four Semifinal Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Michigan holds a marginal but consistent edge in every major efficiency metric over Arizona, with both teams posting elite two-way profiles and identical 31-2 records. The gap is so small (~0.82 KenPom points) that injury context, schedule strength, and market signals become the decisive tiebreakers — all of which lean slightly toward Michigan.
evidence: Michigan: KenPom #2 (AdjEM +38.38, AdjOE 126.7 [8th], AdjDE 88.4 [2nd]); Arizona: KenPom #3 (AdjEM +37.56, AdjOE 127.2 [7th], AdjDE 89.7 [3rd]). Torvik: Michigan #2, Arizona #3. Gap is ~0.82 points — essentially a coin flip on raw metrics.

Yaxel Lendeborg (Michigan) suffered ankle injury in Big Ten Championship but confirmed availability and has played through the tournament. Koa Peat (Arizona) returned from lower-body muscle strain for Big 12 Tournament and made an impact vs. Kansas — appears healthy entering Final Four.

Pre-tournament odds: Michigan -150 to reach Final Four, Arizona -120. Championship odds: Michigan +370, Arizona +380. Market consistently viewed Michigan as marginally stronger throughout the tournament.
Michigan's Big Ten schedule (SOS among nation's toughest) vs. Arizona's Big 12 schedule — both elite, slight edge to Big Ten in depth and overall difficulty.

Michigan beat UMBC, Georgia, Texas Tech (without Toppin), Iowa State (without Gilbert/Momcilovic). Arizona beat LIU, Utah State, Arkansas, Purdue (nation's #1 offense) — Arizona's Elite Eight win over Purdue is the stronger quality win in the bracket.

Michigan's 2nd-ranked defense (AdjDE 88.4) is the toughest assignment Arizona's offense has faced all season. Arizona's 3rd-ranked defense is the toughest Michigan's offense has faced. No significant matchup asymmetry exists — this is a true clash of equals where Michigan's fractional edge is the deciding factor.

result.text: Depth concerns popping up for Arizona Wildcats men’s basketball as toughest stretch of schedule continues | Arizona Desert Swarm Skip to main content SB Nation SB Nation logo An Arizona Wildcats Community Arizona Desert Swarm March 17, 2026 Arizona Desert Swarm Notifications Notifications The Feed Sections Arizona Basketball Arizona Football Arizona Softball Arizona Recruiting Arizona Soccer Arizona Volleyball Arizona Baseball Arizona Golf Arizona Gymnastics Arizona in the Pros Betting FanDuel College Football Odds FanDuel College Basketball Odds Arizona Football Odds Arizona Basketball Odds College Football Picks and Predictions College Basketball Picks and Predictions Shop About Navigation Drawer Login / Sign Up close Close Search The Feed Sections Expand Arizona Basketball Arizona Football Arizona Softball Arizona Recruiting Arizona Soccer Arizona Volleyball Arizona Baseball Arizona Golf Arizona Gymnastics Arizona in the Pros Betting Expand FanDuel College Football Odds FanDuel College Basketball Odds Arizona Football Odds Arizona Basketball Odds College Football Picks and Predictions College Basketball Picks and Predictions Shop About Community Guidelines Newsletters All communities Facebook Twitter Youtube RSS Arizona Desert Swarm Depth concerns popping up for Arizona men’s basketball as toughest stretch of schedule continues Notifications Notifications Arizona Basketball Depth concerns popping up for Arizona men’s basketball as toughest stretch of schedule continues by Brian Pedersen Feb 16, 2026, 10:00 AM UTC Facebook Link TUCSON, ARIZONA - FEBRUARY 14: Motiejus Krivas #13 and Koa Peat #10 of the Arizona Wildcats walk off the court after losing to the Texas Tech Red Raiders 78-75 in overtime at McKale Center at ALKEME Arena on February 14, 2026 in Tucson, Arizona. (Photo by Chris Coduto/Getty Images) Getty Images In Tommy Lloyd’s first season, it was Kerr Kriisa . The second year it was Oumar Ballo. Then Henri Veesaar in 2023-24 and Motiejus Krivas last season. Injuries and absences are a part of every sport, and Arizona has not been immune from this. It just hadn’t happened to the current Wildcats team until Saturday, when Dwayne Aristode sat out the overtime loss to Texas Tech with an illness and Koa Peat suffered a lower body injury and missed the second half. “If you think you’re going to go through a season unscathed, you’ve never done this before,” Lloyd said Saturday after the UA lost a second straight game. “You just got to be honest with yourself. We’re all human. I mean, the season’s been long, obviously you can see some injuries and illnesses are starting to pile up and catch up with us a little bit. We’ll bounce back. And hopefully we will be fully loaded when we need to be.” Both Aristode and Peat could miss the next game, Wednesday at home against No. 22 BYU. result.snippet: The Michigan Wolverines appear to have avoided a major injury scare just days before the start of the 2026 NCAA Men's Basketball Tournament. According to reporter Alejandro Zúñiga, Michigan forward Yaxel Lendeborg revealed that he is dealing with a low ankle sprain, but he expects to play in the Wolverines' NCAA Tournament opener.

result.text: Depth concerns popping up for Arizona Wildcats men’s basketball as toughest stretch of schedule continues | Arizona Desert Swarm Skip to main content SB Nation SB Nation logo An Arizona Wildcats Community Arizona Desert Swarm March 17, 2026 Arizona Desert Swarm Notifications Notifications The Feed Sections Arizona Basketball Arizona Football Arizona Softball Arizona Recruiting Arizona Soccer Arizona Volleyball Arizona Baseball Arizona Golf Arizona Gymnastics Arizona in the Pros Betting FanDuel College Football Odds FanDuel College Basketball Odds Arizona Football Odds Arizona Basketball Odds College Football Picks and Predictions College Basketball Picks and Predictions Shop About Navigation Drawer Login / Sign Up close Close Search The Feed Sections Expand Arizona Basketball Arizona Football Arizona Softball Arizona Recruiting Arizona Soccer Arizona Volleyball Arizona Baseball Arizona Golf Arizona Gymnastics Arizona in the Pros Betting Expand FanDuel College Football Odds FanDuel College Basketball Odds Arizona Football Odds Arizona Basketball Odds College Football Picks and Predictions College Basketball Picks and Predictions Shop About Community Guidelines Newsletters All communities Facebook Twitter Youtube RSS Arizona Desert Swarm Depth concerns popping up for Arizona men’s basketball as toughest stretch of schedule continues Notifications Notifications Arizona Basketball Depth concerns popping up for Arizona men’s basketball as toughest stretch of schedule continues by Brian Pedersen Feb 16, 2026, 10:00 AM UTC Facebook Link TUCSON, ARIZONA - FEBRUARY 14: Motiejus Krivas #13 and Koa Peat #10 of the Arizona Wildcats walk off the court after losing to the Texas Tech Red Raiders 78-75 in overtime at McKale Center at ALKEME Arena on February 14, 2026 in Tucson, Arizona. (Photo by Chris Coduto/Getty Images) Getty Images In Tommy Lloyd’s first season, it was Kerr Kriisa . The second year it was Oumar Ballo. Then Henri Veesaar in 2023-24 and Motiejus Krivas last season. Injuries and absences are a part of every sport, and Arizona has not been immune from this. It just hadn’t happened to the current Wildcats team until Saturday, when Dwayne Aristode sat out the overtime loss to Texas Tech with an illness and Koa Peat suffered a lower body injury and missed the second half. “If you think you’re going to go through a season unscathed, you’ve never done this before,” Lloyd said Saturday after the UA lost a second straight game. “You just got to be honest with yourself. We’re all human. I mean, the season’s been long, obviously you can see some injuries and illnesses are starting to pile up and catch up with us a little bit. We’ll bounce back. And hopefully we will be fully loaded when we need to be.” Both Aristode and Peat could miss the next game, Wednesday at home against No. 22 BYU. If Peat can’t go it will mark the first time this season the UA will send out a different starting five, but more importantly it means the 8-man rotation that has served

Yaxel Lendeborg leaves no NCAA Tournament doubt after ankle injury vs. Purdue Skip to main content About Michigan Wolverines News Michigan Football Michigan Basketball FanSided NCAA Sites Follow Us Share on X About Michigan Wolverines News Michigan Football Michigan Football Michigan Football All-Time Lists Michigan Football Recruiting Michigan Basketball Michigan Basketball Michigan Basketball All-Time Lists Michigan Basketball Recruiting Michigan Women's Basketball FanSided NCAA Sites Share on X Yaxel Lendeborg leaves no NCAA Tournament doubt after ankle injury vs. Purdue Yaxel Lendeborg made it abundantly clear if his injury is going to keep him out of March Madness. By John Buhler | Share on Facebook Share on X Share on Reddit Copy Article Add us as a preferred source Yaxel Lendeborg, Michigan Wolverines | Junfu Han / USA TODAY NETWORK via Imagn Images The Michigan Wolverines may have earned a No. 1 seed in the NCAA Tournament, but it did not go according to plan for them in the Big Ten Championship. Not only did they fall in the conference title bout to Purdue, but star forward Yaxel Lendeborg suffered a scary ankle injury . Michigan has a loaded roster, but he has been the guy Dusty May has gone to all season long to carry the team in the clutch. --> The Michigan Wolverines may have earned a No. 1 seed in the NCAA Tournament, but it did not go according to plan for them in the Big Ten Championship. Not only did they fall in the conference title bout to Purdue, but star forward Yaxel Lendeborg suffered a scary ankle injury . Michigan has a loaded roster, but he has been the guy Dusty May has gone to all season long to carry the team in the clutch. Thankfully, it does not look like this ankle injury will be keeping him out of the NCAA Tournament... --> Thankfully, it does not look like this ankle injury will be keeping him out of the NCAA Tournament... Michigan forward Yaxel Lendeborg told me he has a low ankle-sprain and expects to play in the Wolverines’ NCAA Tournament opener. — Alejandro Zúñiga (@ByAZuniga) March 15, 2026 Michigan earned the No. 1 seed in the Midwest Region. The Wolverines will take on the the winner of the UMBC Retrievers and the Howard Bison in their First Four game to determine the No. 16 seed. Frankly, Lendeborg does not need to play very much at all in that first-round game. result.title: Arizona's Koa Peat Makes Impact in Return From Injury
result.snippet: Arizona forward Koa Peat made his return in an emphatic way as the Wildcats cruised to an 84-61 win over the Kansas Jayhawks at home. Peat had missed Arizona's previous three games after suffering a lower body injury against Texas Tech on Feb. 14, but his return has raised the ceiling for one of the best teams in the country heading into the NCAA Tournament .

result.title: Arizona star Koa Peat ruled out for major upcoming games
result.snippet: Arizona freshman forward Koa Peat will miss at least the Wildcats' next two games against No. 23 BYU and No. 2 Houston as he recovers from a muscle strain in his lower leg, the team announced ...

result.title: Star Arizona freshman Koa Peat sidelined with muscle strain in lower ...
result.snippet: Arizona announced that star freshman Koa Peat has a muscle strain in his lower leg. He'll be re-evaluated next week without a precise timeline to return.

result.title: College Basketball 2025-2026 Buzz: Koa Peat Injury Update
result.snippet: Arizona forward Koa Peat sustained a lower body injury , causing him to miss the second half and overtime of the Wildcats' loss to Texas Tech on Saturday. Arizona head coach Tommy Lloyd did not provide an update regarding Peat's status . The freshman is averaging 13.8 points per game and 5.4 rebounds. modelTrace.content: {"pick":{"gameId":"f4-2","winnerId":"michigan","confidence":0.54,"rationale":"Michigan and Arizona are separated by less than one point in adjusted efficiency margin (KenPom #2 at +38.38 vs. #3 at +37.56, Torvik #2 vs. #3), with both teams posting identical 31-2 records and elite two-way profiles — Michigan's 2nd-ranked defense (AdjDE 88.4) marginally superior to Arizona's 3rd-ranked unit (AdjDE 89.7), and both offenses ranked 7th-8th nationally. The tiebreakers that tip this razor-thin pick to Michigan are the Wolverines' fractionally superior efficiency on both ends of the floor, the Big Ten's slight schedule-strength edge over the Big 12, and the pre-tournament betting market's consistent lean toward Michigan (-150 vs. Arizona -120 to reach the Final Four). Yaxel Lendeborg's ankle injury is a genuine concern but he has played through the tournament, while Koa Peat's return from his lower-body strain keeps Arizona fully dangerous — making this effectively a coin-flip game where Michigan's marginal efficiency edge and Dusty May's elite defensive system provide the narrowest of margins."},"reasoningStep":{"id":"f4-2-analysis","title":"Arizona vs. Michigan Final Four Semifinal Analysis","summary":"Michigan holds a marginal but consistent edge in every major efficiency metric over Arizona, with both teams posting elite two-way profiles and identical 31-2 records. The gap is so small (~0.82 KenPom points) that injury context, schedule strength, and market signals become the decisive tiebreakers — all of which lean slightly toward Michigan.","evidence":["Michigan: KenPom #2 (AdjEM +38.38, AdjOE 126.7 [8th], AdjDE 88.4 [2nd]); Arizona: KenPom #3 (AdjEM +37.56, AdjOE 127.2 [7th], AdjDE 89.7 [3rd]). Torvik: Michigan #2, Arizona #3. Gap is ~0.82 points — essentially a coin flip on raw metrics.","Yaxel Lendeborg (Michigan) suffered ankle injury in Big Ten Championship but confirmed availability and has played through the tournament. Koa Peat (Arizona) returned from lower-body muscle strain for Big 12 Tournament and made an impact vs. Kansas — appears healthy entering Final Four.","Pre-tournament odds: Michigan -150 to reach Final Four, Arizona -120. Championship odds: Michigan +370, Arizona +380. Market consistently viewed Michigan as marginally stronger throughout the tournament.","Michigan's Big Ten schedule (SOS among nation's toughest) vs. Arizona's Big 12 schedule — both elite, slight edge to Big Ten in depth and overall difficulty.","Michigan beat UMBC, Georgia, Texas Tech (without Toppin), Iowa State (without Gilbert/Momcilovic). Arizona beat LIU, Utah State, Arkansas, Purdue (nation's #1 offense) — Arizona's Elite Eight win over Purdue is the stronger quality win in the bracket.","Michigan's 2nd-ranked defense (AdjDE 88.4) is the toughest assignment Arizona's offense has faced all season. Arizona's 3rd-ranked defense is the toughest Michigan's offense has faced. No significant matchup asymmetry exists — this is a true clash of equals where Michigan's fractional edge is the deciding factor."]}}
